target.founder: Julie Böhning (CEO, co-founded Gruyère Space Program at EPFL), Jérémy Marciacq (CTO, co-founded Gruyère Space Program at EPFL)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
PAVE Space is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Julie Böhning and Jérémy Marciacq, both formerly of the Gruyère Space Program at EPFL. The company has raised $40M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 5 public Space & Aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Stage-progression analysis is the only applicable valuation method given data constraints. Using total capital raised of $40M as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for progression from seed to the next institutional stage yields an implied valuation of $100M. A sensitivity band around this central estimate—accounting for variance in comparable stage-ups and execution risk—supports a range of $85M to $115M.

Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because PAVE Space's revenue is not disclosed; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S of 25.8x across the 6 public names remains inaccessible without this input. Peer-median methods failed due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable data within the narrow private set. The $85M–$115M range, centered at $100M, is therefore supported by a single method. Key uncertainties include lack of disclosed financial metrics, early-stage execution risk, and the wide performance dispersion typical of seed-stage space ventures.
